biomes.name: Temperate Forest
biomes.description: Rolling woodlands of oak, ash and elm cloaking the hills. Settlements cluster near rivers, and old paths wind beneath dense canopies.

climate.notes: River floodplains and valley bottoms accumulate moisture and support forested wetlands.

biomes.name: Boreal Forest
biomes.description: Dense evergreen wilderness of pine and spruce stretching to the horizon. Winters are harsh, settlements sparse, and the silence between the trees is absolute.

climate.notes: Despite low precipitation, poor drainage over frozen ground creates the world's largest wetland complex — vast peat bogs and muskegs covering up to 30% of the landscape.

biomes.name: Plains & Farmlands
biomes.description: Vast open grasslands and cultivated fields broken by hedgerows and market towns. The richest land draws the densest populations — and the most conflict.

climate.temperatureRange: Extreme seasonality: −20 °C to +30 °C; severe winters, hot summers
climate.notes: Prairie potholes and seasonal river marshes form where the land dips. Wetlands are scattered but not dominant.

biomes.name: Highlands
biomes.description: Rugged elevated terrain of exposed rock, heather-clad plateaus and deep glacial valleys. Hardy folk scratch out lives in fortified holds and cliff-edge villages.

climate.temperatureRange: 4–12 °C average; long harsh winters, short summers
climate.notes: High drainage density keeps most slopes dry, but corries and plateau basins trap water forming upland bogs and tarns.

biomes.name: Desert
biomes.description: An immense expanse of baking sun and shifting dunes where water is the only true currency. Razor-edged ridges and dried gulches baked the colour of rust break the horizon. Ancient ruins and buried cities outnumber the living settlements — only the desperate, the hunted and the dangerously stubborn put down roots here.

climate.temperatureRange: Daytime 38–49 °C; nights near freezing; extreme diurnal range
climate.notes: Essentially no permanent wetlands. Rare oases form where aquifers reach the surface. High drainage density strips water quickly — terrain too steep and eroded for water to pool.

biomes.name: Arctic Tundra
biomes.description: A treeless frozen expanse where permafrost lies inches below the soil and blizzards strike without warning. Only the toughest cultures survive here, buried in fur and stone.

climate.notes: Low precipitation but impermeable permafrost prevents drainage — low areas fill with standing water creating widespread bogs and polygon ponds.

biomes.name: Swampland
biomes.description: Waterlogged lowlands choked with rotting vegetation and slow black rivers. What lives here has adapted to the damp and the dark, and does not welcome visitors.

climate.precipitationMm: 1 000–2 000 mm/year (or fed by adjacent river flooding)
climate.notes: Standing water, anaerobic soils and seasonal flooding define this biome. Wetlands are not a feature here — they are the landscape.

biomes.name: Moorland
biomes.description: Open upland covered in heather, gorse and bracken, cut by shallow streams and scarred by ancient peat workings. Mist and fog are permanent residents.

climate.temperatureRange: 5–15 °C average; mild oceanic winters, cool misty summers
climate.notes: Persistent moisture and acidic soils build deep peat. Blanket bog is widespread, covering large plateau areas.

biomes.name: Rainforest
biomes.description: A cathedral of ancient trees so dense the canopy blocks the sky. Rivers run warm and brown. Life is extravagant and lethal in equal measure.

climate.temperatureRange: 20–27 °C average; no strong seasonality; high humidity year-round
climate.notes: Lowland areas flood seasonally (várzea). Forested wetlands and flooded forests are common in valley bottoms and river margins.

biomes.name: Savanna
biomes.description: Sweeping grasslands studded with flat-topped acacia and broken by seasonal rivers. Great beasts roam in herds. Settlements are fortified against both raid and stampede.

climate.temperatureRange: Dry season 10–20 °C; wet season 20–30 °C; strongly seasonal
climate.notes: Wet season brings seasonal marshes and ephemeral pans. Permanent wetlands rare except along major river floodplains.

biomes.name: Coastal & Islands
biomes.description: A fractured shoreline of coves, cliffs and island chains where every community looks seaward. Trade routes, fishing fleets and piracy are all equally common.

climate.temperatureRange: Mild oceanic; 8–20 °C average; ocean moderates extremes
climate.notes: Estuaries, tidal marshes and mangroves form where rivers meet the sea. Salt marshes are a defining coastal feature.

biomes.name: Underdark
biomes.description: A vast subterranean world of caverns, underground seas and fungal forests stretching miles beneath the surface. Where surface folk see only darkness, the Underdark's denizens see a world lit by faerzress glow and bioluminescent life. Ancient cities of incomprehensible cruelty are separated by wildernesses of tunnel, ruin and monster lair that few survive crossing.

geology.notes: No weather. Air quality is a constant hazard — volcanic gas and oxygen depletion pool in low passages. Faerzress radiation suffuses most inhabited regions, blocking divination and disrupting teleportation.
